90	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
91	type BeadEventAppender interface {
92		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
93	}
94	
95	// ExecuteBeadOptions holds all parameters for an execute-bead worker run.
96	type ExecuteBeadOptions struct {
97		FromRev       string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
98		Harness       string
99		Model         string
100		Provider      string // explicit provider name; passed through to agent as -provider
101		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref; passed through to agent as -model-ref
102		Effort        string
103		ContextBudget string // prompt budget: "", "minimal" (omits large governing docs for cheap-tier)
104		PromptFile    string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
105		WorkerID      string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
106		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, receives a kind:routing evidence entry after
107		// the agent run completes. This is the hook that feeds the cost-tiered
108		// routing analytics described in FEAT-routing-visibility.
109		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
110		// MirrorCfg, when non-nil and configured, enables mirroring the
111		// finalized .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ bundle to an out-of-band archive
112		// after the worker writes the result. Failures never affect the bead
113		// outcome — see executions_mirror.go.
114		MirrorCfg *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig
115		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
116		// agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ExecuteBead
117		// constructs a fresh service from projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
118		// Tests may inject a pre-built service to avoid loading provider config.
119		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
120		// AgentRunner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path for
121		// the agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil. Tests use this
122		// seam to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real service
123		// or provider chain. When set, it takes precedence over Service.
124		AgentRunner AgentRunner
125	}
126	
127	// ExecuteBeadRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadOptions.
128	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
129	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) are stripped — they live on
130	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's
131	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
132	// runtime intent remain.
133	//
134	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
135	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
136		FromRev     string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
137		PromptFile  string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
138		WorkerID    string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
139		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
140		Service     agentlib.DdxAgent
141		AgentRunner AgentRunner
142	}
143	
144	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBead. It
145	// accepts a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
146	// ExecuteBeadRuntime (plumbing + per-invocation intent), assembles an
147	// equivalent ExecuteBeadOptions, and delegates to ExecuteBead.
148	// Behavior is identical to ExecuteBead with an equivalently-populated
149	// ExecuteBeadOptions.
150	//
151	// Stage 3 of SD-024: this function exists alongside ExecuteBead;
152	// production callers have not migrated yet.
153	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
154		opts := ExecuteBeadOptions{
155			FromRev:       runtime.FromRev,
156			Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
157			Model:         rcfg.Model(),
158			Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
159			ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
160			Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
161			ContextBudget: rcfg.ContextBudget(),
162			PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
163			WorkerID:      runtime.WorkerID,
164			BeadEvents:    runtime.BeadEvents,
165			MirrorCfg:     rcfg.MirrorConfig(),
166			Service:       runtime.Service,
167			AgentRunner:   runtime.AgentRunner,
168		}
169		return ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, opts, gitOps)
170	}
171	
172	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
173	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
174	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
175	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
176	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
177	// worktree.
178	type GitOps interface {
179		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
180		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
181		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
182		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
183		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
184		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
185		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
186		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
187		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
188		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
189		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
190		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
191		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
192		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
193		// other work that might prune it.
194		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
195		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
196		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
197		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
198	}
199	
200	// AgentRunner runs an agent with the given options.
201	type AgentRunner interface {
202		Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)
203	}
204	
205	// Artifact paths for an execute-bead attempt.
206	type executeBeadArtifacts struct {
207		DirAbs      string
208		DirRel      string
209		PromptAbs   string
210		PromptRel   string
211		ManifestAbs string
212		ManifestRel string
213		ResultAbs   string
214		ResultRel   string
215		ChecksAbs   string
216		ChecksRel   string
217		UsageAbs    string
218		UsageRel    string
219	}
220	
221	type executeBeadManifest struct {
222		AttemptID string                    `json:"attempt_id"`
223		WorkerID  string                    `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
224		BeadID    string                    `json:"bead_id"`
225		BaseRev   string                    `json:"base_rev"`
226		CreatedAt time.Time                 `json:"created_at"`
227		Requested executeBeadRequested      `json:"requested"`
228		Bead      executeBeadManifestBead   `json:"bead"`
229		Governing []executeBeadGoverningRef `json:"governing,omitempty"`
230		Paths     executeBeadArtifactPaths  `json:"paths"`
231	}
232	
233	type executeBeadRequested struct {
234		Harness  string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
235		Model    string `json:"model,omitempty"`
236		Provider string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
237		ModelRef string `json:"model_ref,omitempty"`
238		Effort   string `json:"effort,omitempty"`
239		Prompt   string `json:"prompt,omitempty"`
240	}
241	
242	type executeBeadManifestBead struct {
243		ID          string         `json:"id"`
244		Title       string         `json:"title"`
245		Description string         `json:"description,omitempty"`
246		Acceptance  string         `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
247		Parent      string         `json:"parent,omitempty"`
248		Labels      []string       `json:"labels,omitempty"`
249		Metadata    map[string]any `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
250	}
251	
252	type executeBeadGoverningRef struct {
253		ID    string `json:"id"`
254		Path  string `json:"path"`
255		Title string `json:"title,omitempty"`
256	}
257	
258	// GoverningRef is the exported alias for executeBeadGoverningRef, for use
259	// outside the agent package (e.g. cmd/bead_review.go).
260	type GoverningRef = executeBeadGoverningRef
261	
262	type executeBeadArtifactPaths struct {
263		Dir      string `json:"dir"`
264		Prompt   string `json:"prompt"`
265		Manifest string `json:"manifest"`
266		Result   string `json:"result"`
267		Checks   string `json:"checks,omitempty"`
268		Usage    string `json:"usage,omitempty"`
269		Worktree string `json:"worktree"`
270	}
271	
272	// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.
273	const (
274		ExecuteBeadWtDir       = ".ddx" // legacy; kept for compatibility
275		ExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = ".execute-bead-wt-"
276		ExecuteBeadArtifactDir = ".ddx/executions"
277	
278		// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which
279		// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside
280		// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running
281		// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's
282		// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.
283		ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = "ddx-exec-wt"
284	)
285	
286	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
287	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
288	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
289		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")
290		if base == "" {
291			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
292		}
293		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
294	}
295	
296	// RealGitOps implements GitOps via os/exec git commands.
297	type RealGitOps struct{}
298	
299	func (r *RealGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
300		return r.ResolveRev(dir, "HEAD")
301	}
302	
303	func (r *RealGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
304		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", rev).Output()
305		if err != nil {
306			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", rev, err)
307		}
308		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
309	}

1	package agent

3	import (
4		"context"

9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)
14	
15	func TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceRecorded(t *testing.T) {
16		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
17		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
18		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
19	
20		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
21		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{"no-op"})
22	
23		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
24		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
25		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
26	
27		res, err := ExecuteBead(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
28			Harness:     "script",
29			Model:       dirFile,
30			BeadEvents:  beadStore,
31			AgentRunner: runner,
32		}, gitOps)
33	
34		require.NoError(t, err)
35		require.NotNil(t, res)
36	
37		events, evErr := beadStore.Events(beadID)
38		require.NoError(t, evErr)
39	
40		var routingEvt *bead.BeadEvent
41		for i := range events {
42			if events[i].Kind == "routing" {
43				routingEvt = &events[i]
44				break
45			}
46		}
47		require.NotNil(t, routingEvt, "expected a kind=routing evidence event on bead %s", beadID)
48	
49		var body map[string]any
50		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvt.Body), &body),
51			"routing evidence body must be valid JSON")
52	
53		assert.Equal(t, "routing", routingEvt.Kind)
54		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["resolved_provider"], "resolved_provider must be set")
55		assert.Contains(t, body, "route_reason", "route_reason field must be present")
56		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["route_reason"], "route_reason must be non-empty")
57		assert.Contains(t, body, "fallback_chain", "fallback_chain field must be present")
58		assert.Contains(t, body, "resolved_model", "resolved_model field must be present")
59	
60		assert.Equal(t, "script", body["resolved_provider"])
61		assert.Equal(t, "direct-override", body["route_reason"])
62	}
63	
64	func TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceNoAppenderIsNoop(t *testing.T) {
65		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
66		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
67	
68		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
69		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{"no-op"})
70	
71		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
72		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
73	
74		res, err := ExecuteBead(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
75			Harness:     "script",
76			Model:       dirFile,
77			BeadEvents:  nil,
78			AgentRunner: runner,
79		}, gitOps)
80	
81		require.NoError(t, err)
82		require.NotNil(t, res)
83		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges, res.Outcome)
84	}
85	
86	func TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceWithCommit(t *testing.T) {
87		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
88		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
89		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
90	
91		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
92		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
93			"append-line output.txt routing-evidence-test",
94			"commit chore: routing evidence test",
95		})
96	
97		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
98		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
99		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
100		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
101	
102		res, err := ExecuteBead(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
103			Harness:     "script",
104			Model:       dirFile,
105			BeadEvents:  beadStore,
106			AgentRunner: runner,
107		}, gitOps)
108		require.NoError(t, err)
109		require.NotNil(t, res)
110	
111		_, _ = LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
112			LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
113				return Land(projectRoot, BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), RealLandingGitOps{})
114			},
115		})
116	
117		events, evErr := beadStore.Events(beadID)
118		require.NoError(t, evErr)
119	
120		found := false
121		for _, evt := range events {
122			if evt.Kind == "routing" {
123				found = true
124				var body map[string]any
125				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(evt.Body), &body))
126				assert.Equal(t, "script", body["resolved_provider"])
127				assert.Equal(t, "direct-override", body["route_reason"])
128				break
129			}
130		}
131		assert.True(t, found, "routing evidence must be recorded even on task_succeeded path")
132	}
133	
134	func TestAppendLoopRoutingEvidenceRecordsProfileTelemetry(t *testing.T) {
135		app := &stubBeadEventAppender{}
136		appendLoopRoutingEvidence(app, "ddx-0001", ExecuteBeadReport{
137			Provider:         "openai",
138			Model:            "gpt-5.4",
139			RequestedProfile: "default",
140			RequestedTier:    "cheap",
141			ResolvedTier:     "standard",
142			EscalationCount:  1,
143			FinalTier:        "standard",
144		}, time.Date(2026, 4, 21, 16, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
145	
146		require.Len(t, app.events, 1)
147		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-0001", app.events[0].BeadID)
148		assert.Equal(t, "routing", app.events[0].Event.Kind)
149	
150		var body map[string]any
151		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(app.events[0].Event.Body), &body))
152		assert.Equal(t, "default", body["requested_profile"])
153		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", body["requested_tier"])
154		assert.Equal(t, "standard", body["resolved_tier"])
155		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), body["escalation_count"])
156		assert.Equal(t, "standard", body["final_tier"])
157	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"testing"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	)
17	
18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}
59	
60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	// Repo setup
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}
109	
110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	// Directive file helper
112	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	// writeDirectiveFile writes a directive file at path with the given lines.
115	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {
116		t.Helper()
117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755))
118		content := strings.Join(lines, "\n") + "\n"
119		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644))
120	}
121	
122	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
230	// Git assertion helpers
231	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	
233	// gitCommitCount returns the number of commits reachable from ref (plus any
234	// additional git rev-list args like "--not", "SHA").
235	func gitCommitCount(t *testing.T, projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) int {
236		t.Helper()
237		args := append([]string{"rev-list", "--count"}, refAndArgs...)
238		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, args...)
239		n, err := strconv.Atoi(out)
240		require.NoError(t, err, "git rev-list --count %v", refAndArgs)
241		return n
242	}
243	
244	// gitHasMergeCommits returns true when any merge commits exist in ref's history.
245	func gitHasMergeCommits(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
246		t.Helper()
247		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--merges", "--format=%H", ref)
248		return out != ""
249	}
250	
251	// refExists returns true when the given git ref is present in the repo.
252	func refExists(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
253		t.Helper()
254		_, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref)
255		return err == nil
256	}
257	
258	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	// Store helpers
260	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	// makeLoopStore creates an ExecuteBeadLoopStore backed by a bead.Store rooted
263	// in ddxDir. The store is already initialised by newScriptHarnessRepo.
264	func makeLoopStore(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) ExecuteBeadLoopStore {
265		t.Helper()
266		return bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
267	}
268	
269	// countClosedBeads counts how many beads in ddxDir have status "closed".
270	func countClosedBeads(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) int {
271		t.Helper()
272		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
273		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
274		require.NoError(t, err)
275		n := 0
276		for _, b := range all {
277			if b.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
278				n++
279			}
280		}
281		return n
282	}
283	
284	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	// Shell quoting helper
286	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	// escapeForShell escapes a string for embedding in a single-quoted shell argument.
289	func escapeForShell(s string) string {
290		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, "'", `'\''`)
291	}
292

1	package config
2	
3	import "time"
4	
5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;
7	// there are no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
8	//
9	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors.
10	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
11		Assignee                string
12		ReviewMaxRetries        int
13		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
14		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
15		HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
16		Harness                 string
17		Model                   string
18		Profile                 string
19		MinTier                 string
20		MaxTier                 string
21		EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
22	}
23	
24	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
25	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose loop-relevant
26	// accessors return exactly the values supplied in opts.
27	//
28	// Tests must obtain ResolvedConfig via Resolve on the returned *Config
29	// — there is no shortcut to construct a ResolvedConfig directly. Pure
30	// CLI-override fields (Assignee, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are passed
31	// through CLIOverrides at the call site; the constructor stores every
32	// other field on *Config so it round-trips through Resolve.
33	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config {
34		reviewMaxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
35		maxNoChanges := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
36	
37		caps := opts.EvidenceCaps
38	
39		return &Config{
40			Version: "1.0",
41			Agent: &AgentConfig{
42				Harness: opts.Harness,
43				Model:   opts.Model,
44			},
45			Workers: &WorkersConfig{
46				NoProgressCooldown:      opts.NoProgressCooldown.String(),
47				MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: &maxNoChanges,
48				HeartbeatInterval:       opts.HeartbeatInterval.String(),
49			},
50			ReviewMaxRetries: &reviewMaxRetries,
51			EvidenceCaps:     &caps,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestRunConfigOpts names every durable knob the agent run path reads
56	// via ResolvedConfig (per SD-024 §RunOptions → AgentRunRuntime field
57	// classification). Tests must specify each field explicitly; there are
58	// no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
59	//
60	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors and §Stage 2 bead 17.
61	type TestRunConfigOpts struct {
62		Harness       string
63		Model         string
64		Provider      string
65		ModelRef      string
66		Effort        string
67		Permissions   string
68		Timeout       time.Duration
69		WallClock     time.Duration
70		SessionLogDir string
71	}
72	
73	// NewTestConfigForRun returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
74	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose run-path
75	// accessors return the values supplied in opts. Pure CLI-override
76	// fields (Provider, ModelRef, Effort) have no durable home on
77	// AgentConfig and must be applied at Resolve time via CLIOverrides.
78	func NewTestConfigForRun(opts TestRunConfigOpts) *Config {
79		return &Config{
80			Version: "1.0",
81			Agent: &AgentConfig{
82				Harness:       opts.Harness,
83				Model:         opts.Model,
84				TimeoutMS:     int(opts.Timeout / time.Millisecond),
85				SessionLogDir: opts.SessionLogDir,
86				Permissions:   opts.Permissions,
87			},
88		}
89	}
90	
91	// TestBeadConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead worker
92	// reads via ResolvedConfig (per SD-024 §ExecuteBeadOptions →
93	// ExecuteBeadRuntime field classification). Tests must specify each
94	// field explicitly; there are no zero-value defaults that silently
95	// bypass real config.
96	//
97	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors and §Stage 3.
98	type TestBeadConfigOpts struct {
99		Harness  string
100		Model    string
101		Provider string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
102		ModelRef string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
103		Effort   string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
104		Mirror   *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
105	}
106	
107	// NewTestConfigForBead returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with
108	// the matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose
109	// execute-bead-relevant accessors return the values supplied in opts.
110	// Pure CLI-override fields (Provider, ModelRef, Effort) have no
111	// durable home on AgentConfig and must be applied at Resolve time via
112	// CLIOverrides.
113	func NewTestConfigForBead(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) *Config {
114		cfg := &Config{
115			Version: "1.0",
116			Agent: &AgentConfig{
117				Harness: opts.Harness,
118				Model:   opts.Model,
119			},
120		}
121		if opts.Mirror != nil {
122			cfg.Executions = &ExecutionsConfig{Mirror: opts.Mirror}
123		}
124		return cfg
125	}
126	
127	// TestBeadOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
128	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForBead(opts), drive a Resolve call
129	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Provider,
130	// ModelRef, Effort) have no durable home on *Config; they are applied
131	// at Resolve time only.
132	func TestBeadOverrides(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
133		return CLIOverrides{
134			Provider: opts.Provider,
135			ModelRef: opts.ModelRef,
136			Effort:   opts.Effort,
137		}
138	}
139	
140	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
141	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
142	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
143	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
144	// applied at Resolve time only.
145	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
146		return CLIOverrides{
147			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
148			Profile:  opts.Profile,
149			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
150			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
151		}
152	}
